from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, Window, functions as F

PATH = "file:///home/lnc/repos/safe-spark-agents/.worktrees/dev-sweep/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work_seed_s1/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed1337.ndjson"

# type external columns as STRINGS first, validate, then cast (skill: spark-safety §4)
SCHEMA = "order_id STRING, event_time STRING, ts STRING, timestamp STRING, amount STRING, category STRING"


@dp.materialized_view(name="orders_parsed")
def orders_parsed():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    raw = spark.read.text(PATH)                      # column: value
    df = raw.withColumn("j", F.from_json(F.col("value"), SCHEMA))

    event_raw = F.coalesce(F.col("j.event_time"), F.col("j.ts"), F.col("j.timestamp"))
    # branch epoch-millis string vs ISO-8601 string explicitly (skill: spark-safety §3)
    is_epoch = event_raw.rlike("^[0-9]+$")
    ts = F.when(is_epoch, F.timestamp_millis(event_raw.cast("long"))) \
          .otherwise(F.to_timestamp(event_raw))

    # epoch seconds is a tz-independent absolute instant; derive the UTC day arithmetically
    # (avoids any session-timezone mutation, which the gate rejects)
    epoch_sec = ts.cast("long")
    event_date = F.date_add(F.to_date(F.lit("1970-01-01")),
                            F.floor(epoch_sec / F.lit(86400)).cast("int"))

    amount_d = F.col("j.amount").cast("double")
    order_id = F.col("j.order_id")
    category = F.col("j.category")

    # expectations: json parses, key present, amount numeric, UTC date resolvable
    is_valid = (F.col("j").isNotNull()
                & order_id.isNotNull()
                & amount_d.isNotNull()
                & event_date.isNotNull())

    return df.select(
        F.col("value"),
        order_id.alias("order_id"),
        amount_d.alias("amount"),
        category.alias("category"),
        event_date.alias("event_date"),
        epoch_sec.alias("epoch_sec"),
        is_valid.alias("is_valid"),
    )


@dp.materialized_view(name="orders_quarantine")
def orders_quarantine():
    # corrupt / contract-violating rows are kept (not silently dropped)
    return (SparkSession.active().read.table("orders_parsed")
            .where(~F.col("is_valid"))
            .select("value"))


@dp.materialized_view(name="orders_clean")
def orders_clean():
    df = SparkSession.active().read.table("orders_parsed").where(F.col("is_valid"))
    # deterministic dedup on the business key with an explicit sequence key (skill: spark-safety §2)
    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(
        F.col("epoch_sec").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("amount").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("category").asc_nulls_last(),
    )
    return (df.withColumn("_rn", F.row_number().over(w))
              .where(F.col("_rn") == 1)
              .select("order_id", "amount", "category", "event_date"))


@dp.materialized_view(name="silver_orders")
def silver_orders():
    # one row per order_id, carrying amount + category
    return (SparkSession.active().read.table("orders_clean")
            .select("order_id", "amount", "category"))


@dp.materialized_view(name="gold_daily")
def gold_daily():
    df = SparkSession.active().read.table("orders_clean")
    return df.select(
        F.col("event_date"),
        F.col("amount").alias("revenue"),
        F.col("order_id"),
        F.col("amount"),
        F.col("category"),
    )

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, Window, functions as F

DATA = "file:///home/lnc/repos/safe-spark-agents/.worktrees/dev-sweep/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work_seed_s1/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed2718.ndjson"

# All external columns typed as STRING first, then validated/cast (no silent nulls).
SCHEMA = "order_id STRING, event_time STRING, ts STRING, timestamp STRING, amount STRING, category STRING"


def _staged():
    """Parse + normalize + validity-flag the raw feed. Pure, lazy, deterministic.

    NOTE: we do NOT set session.timeZone (that is a forbidden session mutation in a
    declarative pipeline). Instead the UTC calendar day is derived purely by integer
    arithmetic on the epoch instant, which is timezone-independent.
    """
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    raw = spark.read.text(DATA)  # column: value

    j = F.from_json(F.col("value"), SCHEMA)
    df = raw.withColumn("j", j).select(
        F.col("value"),
        F.col("j.order_id").alias("order_id"),
        F.coalesce(F.col("j.event_time"), F.col("j.ts"), F.col("j.timestamp")).alias("event_time"),
        F.col("j.amount").alias("amount_str"),
        F.col("j.category").alias("category"),
        F.col("j").isNotNull().alias("_parsed"),
    )

    # --- UTC event-time normalization ----------------------------------------
    is_epoch = F.col("event_time").rlike("^[0-9]+$")
    epoch_val = F.col("event_time").cast("long")
    # epoch may arrive in millis (>~1e11) or seconds; ISO strings parsed to an instant.
    epoch_sec = (
        F.when(
            is_epoch,
            F.when(epoch_val > F.lit(100000000000), (epoch_val / 1000)).otherwise(epoch_val),
        )
        .otherwise(F.unix_timestamp(F.to_timestamp(F.col("event_time"))))
    )
    df = df.withColumn("epoch_sec", epoch_sec)

    # UTC calendar day = epoch 1970-01-01 + floor(epoch_sec/86400) days (tz-independent).
    epoch_day = F.floor(F.col("epoch_sec") / 86400).cast("int")
    df = df.withColumn("event_date", F.date_add(F.to_date(F.lit("1970-01-01")), epoch_day))

    # --- amount normalization -------------------------------------------------
    amount_clean = F.regexp_replace(F.col("amount_str"), "[^0-9.\\-]", "")
    amount_dbl = F.when(amount_clean.rlike("^-?[0-9]+(\\.[0-9]+)?$"), amount_clean.cast("double"))
    df = df.withColumn("amount", amount_dbl)

    # --- expectations ---------------------------------------------------------
    valid = (
        F.col("_parsed")
        & F.col("order_id").isNotNull()
        & F.col("amount").isNotNull()
        & F.col("event_date").isNotNull()
        & (F.year("event_date") >= F.lit(2000))
        & (F.year("event_date") <= F.lit(2099))
    )
    return df.withColumn("_valid", valid)


@dp.materialized_view(name="orders_quarantine")
def orders_quarantine():
    # Corrupt / unparseable / out-of-range rows are retained (not silently dropped).
    return _staged().where(~F.col("_valid")).select("value")


@dp.materialized_view(name="clean_orders")
def clean_orders():
    # Deterministic dedup: newest instant per order_id, deterministic tiebreak.
    df = _staged().where(F.col("_valid"))
    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(
        F.col("epoch_sec").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("amount").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("category").asc_nulls_last(),
    )
    return (
        df.withColumn("_rn", F.row_number().over(w))
        .where(F.col("_rn") == 1)
        .select("order_id", "amount", "category", "event_date")
    )


@dp.materialized_view(name="silver_orders")
def silver_orders():
    # One row per order_id carrying amount, category.
    return (
        SparkSession.active()
        .read.table("spark_catalog.default.clean_orders")
        .select("order_id", "amount", "category")
    )


@dp.materialized_view(name="gold_daily")
def gold_daily():
    c = SparkSession.active().read.table("spark_catalog.default.clean_orders")
    return c.select(
        F.col("event_date").cast("date").alias("event_date"),
        F.col("amount").alias("revenue"),
        F.col("order_id"),
        F.col("amount"),
        F.col("category"),
    )

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F
from pyspark.sql import Window

DATA = "file:///home/lnc/repos/safe-spark-agents/.worktrees/dev-sweep/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work_seed_s1/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed1337.ndjson"

# Superset schema so we are robust to which field-name variant the messy feed emits.
_JSON_SCHEMA = (
    "order_id STRING, id STRING, oid STRING, "
    "ts STRING, event_time STRING, timestamp STRING, time STRING, event_ts STRING, "
    "amount STRING, amt STRING, price STRING, total STRING, value STRING, "
    "category STRING, cat STRING, segment STRING"
)


@dp.materialized_view(name="bronze_orders")
def bronze_orders():
    # Read line-by-line so genuinely corrupt (non-JSON) lines survive as rows
    # instead of being silently dropped by the JSON reader.
    raw = SparkSession.active().read.text(DATA).where(F.length(F.trim(F.col("value"))) > 0)
    j = F.from_json(F.col("value"), _JSON_SCHEMA)
    return raw.select(
        F.col("value").alias("raw"),
        F.coalesce(j.getField("order_id"), j.getField("id"), j.getField("oid")).alias("order_id_raw"),
        F.coalesce(
            j.getField("ts"), j.getField("event_time"), j.getField("timestamp"),
            j.getField("time"), j.getField("event_ts"),
        ).alias("ts_raw"),
        F.coalesce(
            j.getField("amount"), j.getField("amt"), j.getField("price"),
            j.getField("total"), j.getField("value"),
        ).alias("amount_raw"),
        F.coalesce(j.getField("category"), j.getField("cat"), j.getField("segment")).alias("category"),
    )


@dp.materialized_view(name="norm_orders")
def norm_orders():
    b = SparkSession.active().read.table("spark_catalog.default.bronze_orders")

    # --- amount: strip currency symbols / thousands separators / units ---
    amt_clean = F.regexp_replace(F.col("amount_raw"), r"[^0-9.\-]", "")
    amount = F.when(F.trim(amt_clean) == "", None).otherwise(amt_clean).cast("double")

    # --- event_date as a UTC calendar day, computed WITHOUT session timezone ---
    ts = F.col("ts_raw")
    is_epoch = ts.rlike(r"^[0-9]+$")
    epoch_raw = ts.cast("double")
    # millisecond epochs (the "clearly wrong / far future" case) -> seconds
    epoch_s = F.when(epoch_raw >= F.lit(1.0e12), epoch_raw / F.lit(1000.0)).otherwise(epoch_raw)
    date_from_epoch = F.date_add(F.to_date(F.lit("1970-01-01")), F.floor(epoch_s / F.lit(86400.0)).cast("int"))

    is_datestr = ts.rlike(r"^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}")
    date_from_str = F.to_date(F.substring(ts, 1, 10))  # ISO prefix => unambiguous UTC day

    event_date = (
        F.when(is_epoch, date_from_epoch)
         .when(is_datestr, date_from_str)
         .otherwise(F.lit(None).cast("date"))
    )

    # corrupt lines have no parseable order_id; keep them with a stable synthetic key
    is_corrupt = F.col("order_id_raw").isNull()
    order_id = F.when(
        is_corrupt, F.concat(F.lit("__corrupt__:"), F.sha2(F.col("raw"), 256))
    ).otherwise(F.col("order_id_raw").cast("string"))

    return b.select(
        order_id.alias("order_id"),
        amount.alias("amount"),
        F.col("category").cast("string").alias("category"),
        event_date.alias("event_date"),
        is_corrupt.alias("is_corrupt"),
    )


@dp.materialized_view(name="silver_orders")
def silver_orders():
    # One row per order_id. Deterministic dedupe: prefer the earliest plausible
    # event (valid dates first), tie-broken by amount/category so re-runs are stable.
    n = SparkSession.active().read.table("spark_catalog.default.norm_orders")
    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(
        F.col("event_date").asc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("amount").asc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("category").asc_nulls_last(),
    )
    ranked = n.withColumn("rn", F.row_number().over(w))
    return ranked.where(F.col("rn") == 1).select("order_id", "amount", "category")


@dp.materialized_view(name="gold_daily")
def gold_daily():
    # Trustworthy revenue rows: drop corrupt / undateable / un-amounted lines and
    # implausible far-future dates, then dedupe identically to silver so the two
    # tables stay consistent (same chosen amount/category per shared order_id).
    n = SparkSession.active().read.table("spark_catalog.default.norm_orders")
    valid = n.where(
        (~F.col("is_corrupt"))
        & F.col("event_date").isNotNull()
        & F.col("amount").isNotNull()
        & (F.col("event_date") <= F.to_date(F.lit("2100-01-01")))
    )
    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(
        F.col("event_date").asc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("amount").asc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("category").asc_nulls_last(),
    )
    dedup = valid.withColumn("rn", F.row_number().over(w)).where(F.col("rn") == 1)
    return dedup.select(
        F.col("event_date"),
        F.col("amount").alias("revenue"),
        F.col("order_id"),
        F.col("amount"),
        F.col("category"),
    )

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F, types as T
from pyspark.sql.window import Window

DATA = "file:///home/lnc/repos/safe-spark-agents/.worktrees/dev-sweep/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work_seed_s1/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed2718.ndjson"

CAT = "spark_catalog.default."

# All-string schema: only genuinely unparseable JSON lines land in _corrupt_record,
# instead of well-formed-but-odd values being silently dropped.
SCHEMA = T.StructType([
    T.StructField("order_id", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("event_time", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("amount", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("category", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("_corrupt_record", T.StringType()),
])


@dp.materialized_view(name="bronze_orders")
def bronze_orders():
    return (SparkSession.active().read
            .schema(SCHEMA)
            .option("mode", "PERMISSIVE")
            .option("columnNameOfCorruptRecord", "_corrupt_record")
            .json(DATA))


@dp.materialized_view(name="quarantine_orders")
def quarantine_orders():
    # Corrupt lines must NOT just vanish — keep them here.
    b = SparkSession.active().read.table(CAT + "bronze_orders")
    return b.filter(F.col("_corrupt_record").isNotNull()).select("_corrupt_record")


@dp.materialized_view(name="clean_orders")
def clean_orders():
    b = SparkSession.active().read.table(CAT + "bronze_orders")
    df = b.filter(F.col("_corrupt_record").isNull() & F.col("order_id").isNotNull())

    # Inconsistently-recorded amounts: strip currency/grouping noise, then SAFE cast.
    amount_num = F.expr("try_cast(regexp_replace(amount, '[^0-9.-]', '') as double)")

    # Wrong/garbled times: parse safely (ISO or epoch-seconds), never throw.
    ts = F.coalesce(
        F.expr("try_to_timestamp(event_time)"),
        F.to_timestamp(F.from_unixtime(F.expr("try_cast(event_time as long)"))),
    )

    return (df
            .withColumn("amount_num", amount_num)
            .withColumn("ts", ts)
            .withColumn("ev_date", F.to_date(F.col("ts")))
            # future dates "that can't be real" are not valid for the revenue view
            .withColumn("date_valid",
                        F.col("ts").isNotNull() & (F.to_date(F.col("ts")) <= F.current_date()))
            .select("order_id", "amount_num", "category", "ts", "ev_date", "date_valid"))


@dp.materialized_view(name="silver_orders")
def silver_orders():
    # One canonical row per order_id (dedupe). Prefer rows with a valid date,
    # then earliest timestamp — deterministic so reprocessing is stable.
    c = SparkSession.active().read.table(CAT + "clean_orders")
    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(
        F.col("date_valid").desc(),
        F.col("ts").asc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("amount_num").asc_nulls_last(),
    )
    return (c.withColumn("rn", F.row_number().over(w))
            .filter(F.col("rn") == 1)
            .select(F.col("order_id"),
                    F.col("amount_num").alias("amount"),
                    F.col("category")))


@dp.materialized_view(name="gold_daily")
def gold_daily():
    # Per-order revenue rows on their UTC calendar day, consistent with silver
    # (same order_id -> same amount/category). Only orders with a real date appear.
    c = SparkSession.active().read.table(CAT + "clean_orders")
    s = SparkSession.active().read.table(CAT + "silver_orders")
    dated = (c.filter(F.col("date_valid"))
             .groupBy("order_id")
             .agg(F.min("ev_date").alias("event_date")))
    return (dated.join(s, "order_id")
            .select(F.col("event_date"),
                    F.col("amount").alias("revenue"),
                    F.col("order_id"),
                    F.col("amount"),
                    F.col("category")))

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F

INPUT = "file:///home/lnc/repos/safe-spark-agents/.worktrees/dev-sweep/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work_seed_s1/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed1337.ndjson"

# Read every external column as a STRING first (skill: type-as-string, then validate/cast).
SCHEMA = "order_id string, amount string, event_time string"


def _amount_dbl():
    # text amounts ("12.50", "$1,200.00") must not be dropped: strip non-numeric, then cast.
    amt = F.regexp_replace(F.col("amount"), "[^0-9.\\-]", "")
    return F.when(F.length(amt) > 0, amt.cast("double"))


def _event_date():
    et = F.col("event_time")
    is_epoch = et.rlike("^[0-9]+$")
    epoch_val = et.cast("long")
    # epoch may arrive as seconds (<=10 digits) or millis; normalize to millis.
    epoch_ms = F.when(F.length(et) <= 10, epoch_val * 1000).otherwise(epoch_val)
    # UTC calendar day, fully timezone-independent (no session-conf mutation needed).
    epoch_days = F.floor(epoch_ms / F.lit(86400000)).cast("int")
    date_epoch = F.date_from_unix_date(epoch_days)
    # ISO-8601: the written date is the UTC date for Z/naive-UTC stamps.
    date_iso = F.to_date(F.regexp_extract(et, "^(\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2})", 1), "yyyy-MM-dd")
    return F.when(is_epoch, date_epoch).otherwise(date_iso)


@dp.materialized_view(name="orders_raw")
def orders_raw():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    raw = spark.read.text(INPUT)
    parsed = raw.withColumn("j", F.from_json(F.col("value"), SCHEMA))
    return parsed.select(
        F.col("value"),
        F.col("j.order_id").alias("order_id"),
        F.col("j.amount").alias("amount"),
        F.col("j.event_time").alias("event_time"),
        F.col("j").isNotNull().alias("_parsed"),
    )


@dp.materialized_view(name="orders_quarantine")
def orders_quarantine():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    df = spark.read.table("spark_catalog.default.orders_raw")
    enriched = (df
                .withColumn("revenue_amt", _amount_dbl())
                .withColumn("event_date", _event_date()))
    # rows-in = clean + quarantined; nothing lost silently.
    bad = enriched.where(
        (~F.col("_parsed")) |
        F.col("revenue_amt").isNull() |
        F.col("event_date").isNull())
    return bad.select("value")


@dp.materialized_view(name="orders_validated")
def orders_validated():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    df = spark.read.table("spark_catalog.default.orders_raw")
    enriched = (df
                .withColumn("revenue_amt", _amount_dbl())
                .withColumn("event_date", _event_date()))
    valid = enriched.where(
        F.col("_parsed") &
        F.col("revenue_amt").isNotNull() &
        F.col("event_date").isNotNull())
    return valid.select("order_id", "event_date", "revenue_amt")


@dp.materialized_view(name="gold_daily")
def gold_daily():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    df = spark.read.table("spark_catalog.default.orders_validated")
    return (df.groupBy("event_date")
              .agg(F.sum("revenue_amt").alias("revenue"))
              .select(F.col("event_date").cast("date").alias("event_date"),
                      F.col("revenue")))

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F

DATA = (
    "file:///home/lnc/repos/safe-spark-agents/.worktrees/dev-sweep/"
    "experiments/safe_agent_study/.work_seed_s1/_data/"
    "gen_messy_orders_seed1337.ndjson"
)


# ---- RAW: ingest each NDJSON line as opaque text (no schema coupling) -------
@dp.materialized_view(name="orders_raw")
def orders_raw():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    return spark.read.text(DATA).select(F.col("value").alias("raw"))


# ---- VALIDATED: robustly parse messy amounts + multi-format event times -----
@dp.materialized_view(name="orders_validated")
def orders_validated():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    df = spark.read.table("orders_raw")

    # amount may live under several keys and be numeric OR text ("$1,234.50")
    amt_raw = F.coalesce(
        F.get_json_object("raw", "$.amount"),
        F.get_json_object("raw", "$.value"),
        F.get_json_object("raw", "$.total"),
        F.get_json_object("raw", "$.price"),
        F.get_json_object("raw", "$.revenue"),
    )
    df = df.withColumn("amt_raw", amt_raw)
    # strip currency symbols, thousands separators, stray text -> numeric core
    df = df.withColumn(
        "amt_clean", F.regexp_replace(F.col("amt_raw"), r"[^0-9.\-]", "")
    )
    # try_cast never throws CAST_INVALID_INPUT; bad rows become NULL
    df = df.withColumn("amount", F.expr("try_cast(amt_clean as double)"))

    # event time may live under several keys and be ISO, 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss',
    # date-only, or epoch seconds/millis
    ts_raw = F.coalesce(
        F.get_json_object("raw", "$.event_time"),
        F.get_json_object("raw", "$.event_ts"),
        F.get_json_object("raw", "$.timestamp"),
        F.get_json_object("raw", "$.ts"),
        F.get_json_object("raw", "$.order_time"),
        F.get_json_object("raw", "$.created_at"),
        F.get_json_object("raw", "$.time"),
        F.get_json_object("raw", "$.date"),
    )
    df = df.withColumn("ts_raw", ts_raw)
    df = df.withColumn(
        "event_ts",
        F.coalesce(
            F.expr("try_to_timestamp(ts_raw)"),
            F.expr("try_to_timestamp(ts_raw, 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss')"),
            F.expr("try_to_timestamp(ts_raw, \"yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss\")"),
            F.expr("try_to_timestamp(ts_raw, \"yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss'Z'\")"),
            F.expr("try_to_timestamp(ts_raw, 'MM/dd/yyyy')"),
            F.expr("try_to_timestamp(ts_raw, 'yyyy/MM/dd')"),
            F.expr(
                "case when ts_raw rlike '^[0-9]{10}$' "
                "then timestamp_seconds(try_cast(ts_raw as long)) end"
            ),
            F.expr(
                "case when ts_raw rlike '^[0-9]{13}$' "
                "then timestamp_millis(try_cast(ts_raw as long)) end"
            ),
        ),
    )
    df = df.withColumn("event_date", F.to_date(F.col("event_ts")))

    # keep only real, parseable orders (amount + date both resolved)
    return df.select("amount", "event_ts", "event_date").where(
        F.col("amount").isNotNull() & F.col("event_date").isNotNull()
    )


# ---- AGGREGATED: daily UTC revenue total, matching the output contract ------
@dp.materialized_view(name="gold_daily")
def gold_daily():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    df = spark.read.table("orders_validated")
    return (
        df.groupBy("event_date")
        .agg(F.sum("amount").alias("revenue"))
        .select(F.col("event_date").cast("date").alias("event_date"), "revenue")
    )

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F, Window

INPUT = "file:///home/lnc/repos/safe-spark-agents/.worktrees/dev-sweep/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work_seed_s1/_data/gen_customers_cdc_seed1337.ndjson"

CAT = "spark_catalog.default"


def _g(*paths):
    # tolerant extraction: first non-null among candidate json paths
    return F.coalesce(*[F.get_json_object(F.col("value"), p) for p in paths])


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_cdc_events")
def customers_cdc_events():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    raw = spark.read.text(INPUT)

    customer_id = _g("$.customer_id", "$.id", "$.cust_id", "$.customerId")
    op = _g("$.op", "$.operation", "$.change_type", "$.action", "$.event")
    tier = _g("$.tier", "$.customer_tier")
    region = _g("$.region", "$.customer_region")
    seq = _g("$.seq", "$.sequence", "$.sequence_num", "$.seq_num",
             "$.version", "$.lsn", "$.offset")
    ts = _g("$.ts", "$.timestamp", "$.event_ts", "$.event_time", "$.updated_at")

    df = raw.select(
        customer_id.alias("customer_id"),
        op.alias("op"),
        tier.alias("tier"),
        region.alias("region"),
        seq.alias("seq_raw"),
        ts.alias("ts_raw"),
    ).where(F.col("customer_id").isNotNull())

    # explicit, deterministic ordering key (UTC-safe: ordering is timezone-invariant)
    is_epoch = F.col("ts_raw").rlike("^[0-9]+$")
    ts_parsed = F.when(is_epoch, F.timestamp_millis(F.col("ts_raw").cast("long"))) \
                 .otherwise(F.to_timestamp(F.col("ts_raw")))

    df = (df
          .withColumn("_seq", F.col("seq_raw").cast("long"))
          .withColumn("_ts", ts_parsed)
          .withColumn("_op", F.lower(F.trim(F.col("op")))))

    # remove reload duplicates: identical events collapse to one (idempotent reprocessing)
    df = df.dropDuplicates(
        ["customer_id", "_op", "tier", "region", "_seq", "_ts"]
    )
    return df


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_history")
def customers_history():
    df = SparkSession.active().read.table(f"{CAT}.customers_cdc_events")
    w = Window.partitionBy("customer_id").orderBy(
        F.col("_seq").asc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("_ts").asc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("_op").asc_nulls_last(),
    )
    out = df.withColumn("version", F.row_number().over(w))
    return out.select(
        "customer_id",
        "version",
        F.col("op").alias("op"),
        "tier",
        "region",
    )


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_current")
def customers_current():
    df = SparkSession.active().read.table(f"{CAT}.customers_cdc_events")
    w = Window.partitionBy("customer_id").orderBy(
        F.col("_seq").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("_ts").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("_op").desc_nulls_last(),
    )
    latest = (df.withColumn("_rn", F.row_number().over(w))
                .where(F.col("_rn") == 1))

    is_delete = F.col("_op").isin("d", "del", "delete", "r", "remove", "removed", "deleted")
    current = latest.where(~F.coalesce(is_delete, F.lit(False)))

    return current.select("customer_id", "tier", "region")

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, Window, functions as F

INPUT = "file:///home/lnc/repos/safe-spark-agents/.worktrees/dev-sweep/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work_seed_s1/_data/gen_customers_cdc_seed2718.ndjson"

# Type everything as STRING first; validate/cast later (skill: spark-safety §4)
SCHEMA = ("customer_id string, tier string, region string, op string, "
          "seq string, sequence string, version string, "
          "ts string, event_time string, timestamp string")


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_cdc_raw")
def customers_cdc_raw():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    raw = spark.read.text(INPUT)
    return raw.withColumn("j", F.from_json(F.col("value"), SCHEMA)).select("value", "j.*")


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_cdc_parsed")
def customers_cdc_parsed():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    df = spark.read.table("spark_catalog.default.customers_cdc_raw")

    # explicit, deterministic sequence key (skill: spark-safety §2)
    seq = F.coalesce(F.col("seq"), F.col("sequence"), F.col("version")).cast("long")

    # UTC-stable event-time normalization without mutating session conf (skill §3)
    ts_raw = F.coalesce(F.col("ts"), F.col("event_time"), F.col("timestamp"))
    epoch_long = ts_raw.cast("long")
    is_epoch = ts_raw.rlike("^[0-9]+$")
    event_ts = (F.when(is_epoch & (epoch_long > F.lit(100000000000)),
                       F.timestamp_millis(epoch_long))
                 .when(is_epoch, F.timestamp_seconds(epoch_long))
                 .otherwise(F.to_timestamp(ts_raw)))

    is_delete = F.coalesce(F.lower(F.trim(F.col("op"))).startswith("d"), F.lit(False))

    return (df
            .where(F.col("customer_id").isNotNull())
            .withColumn("seq", seq)
            .withColumn("event_ts", event_ts)
            .withColumn("is_delete", is_delete)
            .select("customer_id", "tier", "region", "op", "seq", "event_ts", "is_delete"))


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_history")
def customers_history():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    df = spark.read.table("spark_catalog.default.customers_cdc_parsed")

    # one row per logical version; collapses reload duplicates -> idempotent reprocessing
    df = df.withColumn(
        "_vkey",
        F.coalesce(F.col("seq").cast("string"),
                   F.date_format(F.col("event_ts"), "yyyyMMddHHmmssSSS"),
                   F.concat_ws("|", F.col("op"), F.col("tier"), F.col("region"))))

    w = Window.partitionBy("customer_id", "_vkey").orderBy(
        F.col("event_ts").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("seq").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("op").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("tier").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("region").desc_nulls_last())

    return (df.withColumn("_rn", F.row_number().over(w))
              .where(F.col("_rn") == 1)
              .select("customer_id", "tier", "region", "op", "seq", "event_ts", "is_delete"))


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_current")
def customers_current():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    df = spark.read.table("spark_catalog.default.customers_history")

    # latest real state per customer, deterministic tie-breaks
    w = Window.partitionBy("customer_id").orderBy(
        F.col("seq").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("event_ts").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("op").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("tier").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("region").desc_nulls_last())

    latest = (df.withColumn("_rn", F.row_number().over(w))
                .where(F.col("_rn") == 1))

    # if most-recent event is a removal, the customer is not in current state
    return (latest.where(~F.coalesce(F.col("is_delete"), F.lit(False)))
                  .select("customer_id", "tier", "region"))

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F, types as T
from pyspark.sql.window import Window

PATH = "file:///home/lnc/repos/safe-spark-agents/.worktrees/dev-sweep/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work_seed_s1/_data/gen_customers_cdc_seed1337.ndjson"

# Superset schema: any field absent in a given record parses to NULL (no error),
# letting us normalize across the messy/unknown upstream spelling.
SCHEMA = T.StructType([
    T.StructField("customer_id", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("id",          T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("cust_id",     T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("op",          T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("operation",   T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("change_type", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("_op",         T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("tier",        T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("region",      T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("seq",         T.LongType()),
    T.StructField("sequence",    T.LongType()),
    T.StructField("seq_no",      T.LongType()),
    T.StructField("version",     T.LongType()),
    T.StructField("lsn",         T.LongType()),
    T.StructField("ts",          T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("event_ts",    T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("event_time",  T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("timestamp",   T.StringType()),
])


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_cdc_events")
def customers_cdc_events():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    raw = spark.read.text(PATH)
    j = raw.select(F.from_json(F.col("value"), SCHEMA).alias("d")).select("d.*")

    cid  = F.coalesce("customer_id", "id", "cust_id")
    op   = F.lower(F.coalesce("op", "operation", "change_type", "_op"))
    onum = F.coalesce("seq", "sequence", "seq_no", "version", "lsn").cast("long")
    ostr = F.coalesce("ts", "event_ts", "event_time", "timestamp").cast("string")

    out = (
        j.select(
            cid.alias("customer_id"),
            op.alias("op"),
            F.col("tier"),
            F.col("region"),
            onum.alias("version"),
            ostr.alias("order_str"),
        )
        .where(F.col("customer_id").isNotNull())
        .withColumn("is_delete", F.col("op").rlike("del|remov|^d$|^r$"))
    )
    # Idempotency: collapse identical re-delivered events (nightly reload dupes).
    return out.dropDuplicates(
        ["customer_id", "version", "order_str", "op", "tier", "region"]
    )


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_history")
def customers_history():
    # One row per version (per delivered, de-duplicated CDC event).
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    e = spark.read.table("spark_catalog.default.customers_cdc_events")
    return e.select("customer_id", "version", "op", "tier", "region")


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_current")
def customers_current():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    e = spark.read.table("spark_catalog.default.customers_cdc_events")
    # Most-recent real state per customer, robust to jumbled arrival order.
    w = Window.partitionBy("customer_id").orderBy(
        F.col("version").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("order_str").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("op").desc_nulls_last(),
    )
    latest = (
        e.withColumn("rn", F.row_number().over(w))
        .where(F.col("rn") == 1)
        .where(~F.coalesce(F.col("is_delete"), F.lit(False)))
    )
    return latest.select("customer_id", "tier", "region")

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, Window, functions as F

INPUT = "file:///home/lnc/repos/safe-spark-agents/.worktrees/dev-sweep/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work_seed_s1/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed1337.ndjson"

# Type everything as string first; validate/cast explicitly (no silent nulls).
SCHEMA = "order_id string, category string, amount string, event_time string"

EPOCH_ORIGIN = F.lit("1970-01-01").cast("date")


@dp.materialized_view(name="orders_clean")
def orders_clean():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    raw = spark.read.text(INPUT)
    parsed = raw.withColumn("j", F.from_json(F.col("value"), SCHEMA))
    df = parsed.select("j.*").where(F.col("order_id").isNotNull())

    # --- UTC event-time normalization (no session-tz mutation) ---
    # epoch-millis strings vs ISO-8601 strings, branched explicitly
    is_epoch = F.col("event_time").rlike("^[0-9]+$")
    ts = (
        F.when(is_epoch, F.timestamp_millis(F.col("event_time").cast("long")))
         .otherwise(F.to_timestamp(F.col("event_time")))
    )
    # Casting a timestamp to long gives the absolute instant in epoch seconds,
    # independent of the (deliberately unpinned) session timezone.
    df = df.withColumn("epoch_sec", ts.cast("long"))

    # numeric validity: quote-wrapped/garbage amounts cast to null -> dropped
    df = df.withColumn("amount_num", F.col("amount").cast("double"))

    # expectations: required key + parseable time + parseable amount
    df = df.where(
        F.col("epoch_sec").isNotNull() & F.col("amount_num").isNotNull()
    )

    # --- deterministic dedup on the business key ---
    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(
        F.col("epoch_sec").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("amount_num").desc_nulls_last(),
    )
    df = (
        df.withColumn("_rn", F.row_number().over(w))
          .where(F.col("_rn") == 1)
          .drop("_rn")
    )

    # UTC calendar day via tz-independent epoch arithmetic
    day_num = F.floor(F.col("epoch_sec") / F.lit(86400)).cast("int")
    df = df.withColumn("event_date", F.date_add(EPOCH_ORIGIN, day_num))

    return df.select("order_id", "category", "event_date", "amount_num")


@dp.materialized_view(name="gold_daily")
def gold_daily():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    src = spark.read.table("orders_clean")
    return (
        src.groupBy("event_date")
           .agg(F.sum("amount_num").alias("revenue"))
           .select("event_date", "revenue")
    )

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F, Window

PATH = "file:///home/lnc/repos/safe-spark-agents/.worktrees/dev-sweep/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work_seed_s1/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed2718.ndjson"

# Everything typed as STRING first (skill: type external cols as strings, then validate/cast).
# Include candidate field spellings so we are robust to the upstream naming.
SCHEMA = ("order_id STRING, id STRING, category STRING, cat STRING, "
          "amount STRING, revenue STRING, price STRING, value_amount STRING, "
          "event_time STRING, event_ts STRING, ts STRING, timestamp STRING, "
          "created_at STRING, seq STRING")


def _parsed():
    raw = SparkSession.active().read.text(PATH)
    return raw.withColumn("j", F.from_json(F.col("value"), SCHEMA))


def _amount(c):
    amt_raw = F.coalesce(c("amount"), c("revenue"), c("price"), c("value_amount"))
    # strip currency symbols / commas / whitespace, then cast; true garbage -> null
    amt_clean = F.regexp_replace(amt_raw, "[,$\\s]", "")
    return amt_clean.cast("double")


def _millis(c):
    et = F.coalesce(c("event_time"), c("event_ts"), c("ts"),
                    c("timestamp"), c("created_at"))
    is_epoch = et.rlike("^[0-9]+$")
    # epoch (>=12 digits -> millis, else seconds); ISO/offset string -> instant via cast
    return (F.when(is_epoch & (F.length(et) >= F.lit(12)), et.cast("long"))
             .when(is_epoch, et.cast("long") * F.lit(1000))
             .otherwise(F.to_timestamp(et).cast("long") * F.lit(1000)))


@dp.materialized_view(name="orders_quarantine")
def orders_quarantine():
    p = _parsed()
    c = lambda n: F.col("j").getField(n)
    amt = _amount(c)
    ms = _millis(c)
    oid = F.coalesce(c("order_id"), c("id"))
    bad = amt.isNull() | ms.isNull() | oid.isNull() | F.col("j").isNull()
    return p.where(bad).select("value")


@dp.materialized_view(name="orders_clean")
def orders_clean():
    p = _parsed()
    c = lambda n: F.col("j").getField(n)
    oid = F.coalesce(c("order_id"), c("id"))
    amt = _amount(c)
    ms = _millis(c)
    # UTC calendar day, computed WITHOUT depending on session timezone:
    # days since 1970-01-01 (UTC) -> date_from_unix_date is timezone-independent.
    event_date = F.date_from_unix_date((ms / F.lit(86400000)).cast("int"))

    enriched = (p.select(
                    oid.alias("order_id"),
                    amt.alias("amount_d"),
                    ms.alias("ms"),
                    event_date.alias("event_date"))
                 .where(F.col("order_id").isNotNull()
                        & F.col("amount_d").isNotNull()
                        & F.col("ms").isNotNull()))

    # deterministic dedup on the business key; newest instant wins, amount as tiebreak
    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(
            F.col("ms").desc_nulls_last(),
            F.col("amount_d").desc_nulls_last())
    return (enriched.withColumn("_rn", F.row_number().over(w))
                    .where(F.col("_rn") == 1)
                    .drop("_rn"))

    T.StructField("order_id", T.StringType()),

    T.StructField("timestamp", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("event_time", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("event_timestamp", T.StringType()),

    T.StructField("order_time", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("time", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("amount", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("revenue", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("price", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("total", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("value", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("category", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("cat", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("product_category", T.StringType()),
])


def _epoch_seconds(ts):
    """Resolve a messy timestamp string to a true instant (epoch seconds),
    independent of session timezone."""
    digits = ts.rlike(r"^\s*\d+\s*$")
    num = F.trim(ts).cast("long")
    # numeric epoch: >=13 digits => milliseconds, else seconds
    epoch_num = F.when(digits & (F.length(F.trim(ts)) >= F.lit(13)),
                       (num / F.lit(1000)).cast("long")) \
                 .when(digits, num)
    # string timestamp (ISO, with or without offset) -> instant -> epoch
    epoch_str = F.unix_timestamp(F.to_timestamp(ts))
    return F.coalesce(epoch_num, epoch_str)

    spark = SparkSession.active()

        F.col("event_timestamp"), F.col("created_at"),
        F.col("order_time"), F.col("time"),
    )
    rev_raw = F.coalesce(
        F.col("amount"), F.col("revenue"), F.col("price"),
        F.col("total"), F.col("value"),
    )

    epoch = _epoch_seconds(ts_raw)
    days = F.floor(epoch / F.lit(86400)).cast("int")           # UTC days since epoch
    event_date = F.date_from_unix_date(days)                   # UTC calendar day
    revenue = F.regexp_replace(rev_raw, r"[^0-9.\-]", "").cast("double")

    return (
        df.select(
            event_date.alias("event_date"),
            revenue.alias("revenue"),
        )
        .where(F.col("event_date").isNotNull() & F.col("revenue").isNotNull())
    )

    StructField("ts",         StringType()),

    StructField("timestamp",  StringType()),
    StructField("created_at", StringType()),
    StructField("order_ts",   StringType()),
    StructField("category",   StringType()),

    spark = SparkSession.active()
    df = spark.read.schema(_SCHEMA).json(DATA)

    ts_raw = F.trim(F.coalesce(
        F.col("ts"), F.col("event_time"), F.col("timestamp"),
        F.col("created_at"), F.col("order_ts"),
    ).cast("string"))

    amt_raw = F.coalesce(F.col("amount"), F.col("revenue"), F.col("price")).cast("string")

    # --- deterministic epoch (seconds) ---------------------------------------
    is_epoch = ts_raw.rlike(r"^\d{10,}$")
    # >=13 digits => milliseconds, else seconds
    epoch_num = F.when(F.length(ts_raw) >= F.lit(13),
                       ts_raw.cast("double") / F.lit(1000.0)
                       ).otherwise(ts_raw.cast("double"))

    has_tz = ts_raw.rlike(r"(Z|[+-]\d{2}:?\d{2})$")
    iso = F.regexp_replace(ts_raw, " ", "T")
    # naive timestamps are interpreted as UTC by appending 'Z' (no session-tz dep)
    iso_utc = F.when(has_tz, iso).otherwise(F.concat(iso, F.lit("Z")))
    # cast/parse with explicit offset yields the absolute instant; unix_timestamp
    # of an instant is timezone-independent.
    epoch_str = F.unix_timestamp(F.to_timestamp(iso_utc))

    epoch = F.when(is_epoch, epoch_num).otherwise(epoch_str)

    # UTC calendar day == floor(epoch/86400) days after the unix epoch date.
    days = F.floor(epoch / F.lit(86400.0)).cast("int")
    event_date = F.date_add(F.to_date(F.lit("1970-01-01")), days)

    # --- revenue normalization -----------------------------------------------
    rev = F.regexp_replace(F.trim(amt_raw), r"[^0-9.\-]", "").cast("double")

            event_date.alias("event_date"),

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, Window, functions as F

INPUT = "file:///home/lnc/repos/safe-spark-agents/.worktrees/dev-sweep/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work_seed_s1/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed1337.ndjson"

# Read everything as STRING first; validate then cast (avoid silent nulls).
SCHEMA = (
    "order_id STRING, id STRING, "
    "merchant_id STRING, merchant STRING, "
    "category STRING, "
    "amount STRING, revenue STRING, price STRING, value STRING, "
    "event_time STRING, ts STRING, timestamp STRING, event_ts STRING, "
    "seq STRING, sequence STRING"
)


def _parsed():
    raw = SparkSession.active().read.text(INPUT)        # column: value
    return raw.withColumn("j", F.from_json("value", SCHEMA))


# Candidate column expressions (all fields exist in the json schema, so safe)
_merchant = F.coalesce(F.col("j.merchant_id"), F.col("j.merchant"))
_orderid = F.coalesce(F.col("j.order_id"), F.col("j.id"))
_amt_str = F.coalesce(F.col("j.amount"), F.col("j.revenue"),
                      F.col("j.price"), F.col("j.value"))
_amt_num = F.regexp_replace(_amt_str, "[^0-9.\\-]", "").cast("double")
_event_raw = F.coalesce(F.col("j.event_time"), F.col("j.ts"),
                        F.col("j.timestamp"), F.col("j.event_ts"))
_seq = F.coalesce(F.col("j.seq"), F.col("j.sequence"))


@dp.materialized_view(name="orders_quarantine")
def orders_quarantine():
    # Rows that fail to parse, or lack a merchant key, or lack a usable amount.
    p = _parsed()
    bad = F.col("j").isNull() | _merchant.isNull() | _amt_num.isNull()
    return p.where(bad).select("value")


@dp.materialized_view(name="orders_clean")
def orders_clean():
    p = _parsed()
    df = p.where(F.col("j").isNotNull()).select(
        _orderid.alias("order_id"),
        _merchant.alias("merchant_id"),
        F.col("j.category").alias("category"),
        _amt_num.alias("amount"),
        _event_raw.alias("event_raw"),
        _seq.alias("seq"),
    )

    # Expectations: must have a merchant key and a parseable amount.
    valid = df.where(F.col("merchant_id").isNotNull() & F.col("amount").isNotNull())

    # UTC-safe event-time normalization: branch epoch vs ISO (no session mutation).
    is_epoch = F.col("event_raw").rlike("^[0-9]+$")
    ev = F.when(is_epoch, F.timestamp_millis(F.col("event_raw").cast("long"))) \
          .otherwise(F.to_timestamp("event_raw"))
    valid = valid.withColumn("event_ts", ev)

    # Deterministic dedup per order; rows lacking an order_id stay distinct so
    # they are NOT collapsed (and therefore not lost).
    dk = F.coalesce(
        F.col("order_id"),
        F.concat(F.lit("nk_"), F.monotonically_increasing_id().cast("string")),
    )
    valid = valid.withColumn("_dk", dk)
    w = Window.partitionBy("_dk").orderBy(
        F.col("event_ts").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("seq").desc_nulls_last(),
    )
    deduped = (valid.withColumn("_rn", F.row_number().over(w))
                    .where(F.col("_rn") == 1)
                    .drop("_rn", "_dk"))
    return deduped


@dp.materialized_view(name="merchant_totals")
def merchant_totals():
    # Per-merchant revenue over ALL clean rows, regardless of category, so this
    # view never drifts below a category breakdown that drops null-category rows.
    df = SparkSession.active().read.table("spark_catalog.default.orders_clean")
    return df.groupBy("merchant_id").agg(F.sum("amount").alias("revenue"))

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, Window, functions as F

INPUT = "file:///home/lnc/repos/safe-spark-agents/.worktrees/dev-sweep/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work_seed_s1/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed2718.ndjson"

# Type everything as STRING first (spark-safety): bad values stay observable
# instead of becoming silent nulls. Cover common field spellings so column
# references always resolve.
SCHEMA = (
    "order_id string, merchant_id string, merchant string, category string, "
    "amount string, revenue string, price string, "
    "event_time string, ts string, timestamp string, seq string, version string"
)


def _parsed():
    raw = SparkSession.active().read.text(INPUT)              # column: value
    df = raw.withColumn("j", F.from_json(F.col("value"), SCHEMA))

    amount_raw = F.coalesce(F.col("j.amount"), F.col("j.revenue"), F.col("j.price"))
    # strip currency symbols / thousands separators / quotes, then cast.
    amount_clean = F.regexp_replace(amount_raw, "[^0-9.\\-]", "")
    amount = amount_clean.cast("double")

    merchant = F.coalesce(F.col("j.merchant_id"), F.col("j.merchant"))

    ts_raw = F.coalesce(F.col("j.event_time"), F.col("j.ts"), F.col("j.timestamp"))
    is_epoch = ts_raw.rlike("^[0-9]+$")
    event_ts = F.when(is_epoch, ts_raw.cast("long")) \
                .otherwise(F.unix_timestamp(F.to_timestamp(ts_raw)))

    seq = F.coalesce(F.col("j.seq"), F.col("j.version")).cast("long")

    return df.select(
        F.col("value"),
        F.col("j.order_id").alias("order_id"),
        merchant.alias("merchant_id"),
        F.col("j.category").alias("category"),
        amount.alias("amount"),
        event_ts.alias("event_ts"),
        seq.alias("seq"),
        F.col("j").isNotNull().alias("parsed_ok"),
    )


def _is_clean():
    return F.col("parsed_ok") & F.col("order_id").isNotNull() & F.col("amount").isNotNull()


@dp.materialized_view(name="orders_quarantine")
def orders_quarantine():
    # Rows that failed parse / missing key / non-numeric amount: kept, not dropped.
    return _parsed().where(~_is_clean()).select("value")


@dp.materialized_view(name="orders_clean")
def orders_clean():
    clean = _parsed().where(_is_clean())
    # Deterministic dedup on the business key. Same input -> same survivor:
    # newest event time, then highest sequence, then the raw line as a stable
    # final tiebreaker (no input-ordering dependence).
    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(
        F.col("event_ts").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("seq").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("value").desc(),
    )
    return (clean.withColumn("_rn", F.row_number().over(w))
                 .where(F.col("_rn") == 1)
                 .drop("_rn"))


@dp.materialized_view(name="merchant_totals")
def merchant_totals():
    # Both dashboards must reconcile to this single, complete revenue figure:
    # every clean, deduped order is counted exactly once — including rows whose
    # category is null/odd (the fan-out bug that quietly dropped sales).
    src = SparkSession.active().read.table("spark_catalog.default.orders_clean")
    return src.agg(F.coalesce(F.sum("amount"), F.lit(0.0)).alias("revenue"))

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F, types as T

DATA = ("file:///home/lnc/repos/safe-spark-agents/.worktrees/dev-sweep/"
        "experiments/safe_agent_study/.work_seed_s1/_data/"
        "gen_messy_orders_seed1337.ndjson")

# Explicit superset schema -> no inference (no forced analysis) and every
# referenced column is guaranteed to resolve (missing fields read as null).
_SCHEMA = T.StructType([
    T.StructField("order_id",  T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("merchant_id", T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("merchant",  T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("category",  T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("amount",    T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("revenue",   T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("price",     T.StringType()),
    T.StructField("total",     T.StringType()),
])


def _money(*cols):
    """Coalesce candidate amount fields and parse messy money strings."""
    raw = F.coalesce(*[F.col(c) for c in cols])
    cleaned = F.regexp_replace(raw.cast("string"), r"[^0-9.\-]", "")
    return F.when(cleaned == "", F.lit(None)).otherwise(cleaned.cast("double"))


@dp.materialized_view(name="orders_clean")
def orders_clean():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    df = spark.read.schema(_SCHEMA).json(DATA)
    merchant = F.coalesce(F.col("merchant_id"), F.col("merchant"), F.lit("UNKNOWN"))
    # Keep rows that have no category instead of dropping them -> no lost sales.
    category = F.when(
        (F.col("category").isNull()) | (F.trim(F.col("category")) == ""),
        F.lit("uncategorized"),
    ).otherwise(F.trim(F.col("category")))
    rev = _money("revenue", "amount", "price", "total")
    return df.select(
        merchant.alias("merchant"),
        category.alias("category"),
        F.coalesce(rev, F.lit(0.0)).alias("rev"),
    )


@dp.materialized_view(name="merchant_totals")
def merchant_totals():
    src = SparkSession.active().read.table("orders_clean")
    return (src.groupBy("merchant")
               .agg(F.sum("rev").alias("revenue"))
               .select("merchant", "revenue"))


@dp.materialized_view(name="category_totals")
def category_totals():
    src = SparkSession.active().read.table("orders_clean")
    return (src.groupBy("category")
               .agg(F.sum("rev").alias("revenue"))
               .select("category", "revenue"))

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, Window, functions as F

INPUT = "file:///home/lnc/repos/safe-spark-agents/.worktrees/dev-sweep/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work_seed_s2/_data/gen_customers_cdc_seed1337.ndjson"

# Type everything as string first (validate/cast later), include candidate spellings.
SCHEMA = ", ".join([
    "customer_id string", "id string", "cust_id string",
    "tier string", "region string",
    "op string", "operation string", "_change_type string", "change_type string",
    "seq string", "sequence_num string", "sequence string", "lsn string", "offset string", "_seq string",
    "ts string", "event_time string", "updated_at string", "timestamp string", "event_ts string",
    "__deleted string", "deleted string", "is_deleted string",
])


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_current")
def customers_current():
    raw = SparkSession.active().read.text(INPUT)
    j = raw.withColumn("j", F.from_json("value", SCHEMA)).select("j.*")

    key = F.coalesce("customer_id", "id", "cust_id")
    op = F.lower(F.trim(F.coalesce("op", "operation", "_change_type", "change_type")))
    seq = F.coalesce(
        F.col("seq"), F.col("sequence_num"), F.col("sequence"),
        F.col("lsn"), F.col("offset"), F.col("_seq"),
    ).cast("long")
    ts = F.coalesce("ts", "event_time", "updated_at", "timestamp", "event_ts")

    is_epoch = ts.rlike("^[0-9]+$")
    event_ts = F.when(is_epoch, F.timestamp_millis(ts.cast("long"))) \
                .otherwise(F.to_timestamp(ts))

    deleted_flag = F.coalesce(
        F.coalesce("__deleted", "deleted", "is_deleted").rlike("(?i)^(true|t|1|yes|y)$"),
        F.lit(False),
    )
    op_delete = F.coalesce(op.isin("delete", "d", "remove", "del"), F.lit(False))

    df = (j
          .withColumn("_key", key)
          .withColumn("_op", op)
          .withColumn("_seq", seq)
          .withColumn("_event_ts", event_ts)
          .withColumn("_deleted", op_delete | deleted_flag))

    # Deterministic latest record per customer: newest event-time, then highest sequence.
    w = Window.partitionBy("_key").orderBy(
        F.col("_event_ts").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("_seq").desc_nulls_last(),
    )
    cur = (df.where(F.col("_key").isNotNull())
             .withColumn("_rn", F.row_number().over(w))
             .where(F.col("_rn") == 1)
             .drop("_rn"))
    return cur


@dp.materialized_view(name="customer_segments")
def customer_segments():
    cur = SparkSession.active().read.table("customers_current")
    # Exclude customers whose current state is a delete/removal.
    alive = cur.where(~F.col("_deleted"))
    return (alive
            .groupBy("tier", "region")
            .agg(F.count(F.lit(1)).alias("n"))
            .select("tier", "region", "n"))

INPUT = (

# Type everything as string first (skill: don't let bad values become silent nulls,
# don't declare event-time as TIMESTAMP in the schema). Extra fields just null out.
_SCHEMA = (
    "customer_id string, id string, cust_id string, "
    "tier string, region string, "
    "op string, operation string, change_type string, "
    "seq string, sequence string, version string, lsn string, "
    "event_ts string, event_time string, ts string, updated_at string, "
    "deleted string, is_deleted string"
)


@dp.materialized_view(name="customer_segments")
def customer_segments():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    raw = spark.read.text(INPUT)  # column: value
    j = raw.select(F.from_json(F.col("value"), _SCHEMA).alias("j")).select("j.*")

    # --- business key ---
    cid = F.coalesce("customer_id", "id", "cust_id")

    # --- deterministic sequence key ---
    seq = F.coalesce("seq", "sequence", "version", "lsn").cast("long")

    # --- event-time: handle epoch and ISO explicitly, compare as instants ---
    raw_et = F.coalesce("event_ts", "event_time", "ts", "updated_at")
    is_epoch = raw_et.rlike("^[0-9]+$")
    event_ts = F.when(is_epoch, F.timestamp_millis(raw_et.cast("long"))) \
                .otherwise(F.to_timestamp(raw_et))

    # --- deletion / tombstone detection ---
    op = F.lower(F.coalesce("op", "operation", "change_type"))
    del_flag = F.lower(F.coalesce("deleted", "is_deleted"))
    is_deleted = (
        F.coalesce(op.isin("d", "delete", "del", "remove", "r"), F.lit(False))
        | F.coalesce(del_flag.isin("true", "1", "t", "yes"), F.lit(False))
    )

    enriched = (
        j.withColumn("_cid", cid)
         .withColumn("_seq", seq)
         .withColumn("_event_ts", event_ts)
         .withColumn("_is_deleted", is_deleted)
         .where(F.col("_cid").isNotNull())
    )

    # keep the newest record per customer; deterministic tiebreak on sequence
    w = Window.partitionBy("_cid").orderBy(
        F.col("_event_ts").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("_seq").desc_nulls_last(),
    )
    current = (
        enriched.withColumn("_rn", F.row_number().over(w))
                .where(F.col("_rn") == 1)
                .where(~F.col("_is_deleted"))   # exclude currently-removed customers
    )

    return (
        current.groupBy(
            F.trim(F.col("tier")).alias("tier"),
            F.trim(F.col("region")).alias("region"),
        ).agg(F.count(F.lit(1)).alias("n"))
    )

    df = SparkSession.active().read.table("customers_cdc_raw")

    cust_id = F.coalesce(F.col("id"), F.col("customer_id"))
    op = F.lower(F.trim(F.coalesce(F.col("op"), F.col("__op"),
                                   F.col("operation"), F.lit(""))))
    seq = F.coalesce(F.col("seq"), F.col("sequence"))
    ts = F.coalesce(F.col("ts"), F.col("__ts"), F.lit(""))

    enriched = (
        df.withColumn("_cust_id", cust_id)
          .withColumn("_op", op)
          .withColumn("_seq", seq)
          .withColumn("_ts", ts)
          .where(F.col("_cust_id").isNotNull())
    )

    # Latest event per customer; deterministic tiebreakers for stable reprocessing.
    w = Window.partitionBy("_cust_id").orderBy(
        F.col("_seq").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("_ts").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("_op").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("tier").asc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("region").asc_nulls_last(),
    )

    latest = (
        enriched.withColumn("_rn", F.row_number().over(w))
                .where(F.col("_rn") == 1)
    )

    # Exclude customers whose current state is "removed" (delete op).
    current = latest.where(~F.col("_op").startswith("d"))

    return (
        current.groupBy("tier", "region")
               .agg(F.count(F.lit(1)).alias("n"))
               .select("tier", "region", "n")
    )

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, Window, functions as F

DATA_PATH = "file:///home/lnc/repos/safe-spark-agents/.worktrees/dev-sweep/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work_seed_s2/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed1337.ndjson"

# Type everything as STRING first, then validate/normalize explicitly.
SCHEMA = (
    "order_id STRING, "
    "event_time STRING, ts STRING, timestamp STRING, occurred_at STRING, "
    "created_at STRING, order_time STRING, "
    "amount STRING, revenue STRING, price STRING, total STRING"
)


@dp.materialized_view(name="orders_clean")
def orders_clean():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    raw = spark.read.text(DATA_PATH)

    j = F.from_json(F.col("value"), SCHEMA)
    df = raw.select(F.col("value"), j.alias("j")).select("value", "j.*")

    # Coalesce the various possible event-time / amount field spellings.
    event_raw = F.coalesce(
        F.col("event_time"), F.col("ts"), F.col("timestamp"),
        F.col("occurred_at"), F.col("created_at"), F.col("order_time"),
    )
    amount_raw = F.coalesce(
        F.col("amount"), F.col("revenue"), F.col("price"), F.col("total"),
    )

    df = df.withColumn("event_raw", event_raw).withColumn("amount_raw", amount_raw)

    # --- UTC-correct event timestamp ---
    # Epoch numeric (seconds vs millis) is inherently UTC; ISO strings carry their
    # own offset. Branch on the format rather than trusting one parser.
    is_epoch = F.col("event_raw").rlike("^[0-9]+$")
    epoch_ts = F.when(
        F.length(F.col("event_raw")) >= F.lit(13),
        F.timestamp_millis(F.col("event_raw").cast("long")),
    ).otherwise(
        F.timestamp_seconds(F.col("event_raw").cast("long"))
    )
    event_ts = F.when(is_epoch, epoch_ts).otherwise(F.to_timestamp(F.col("event_raw")))
    df = df.withColumn("event_ts", event_ts)

    # Derive the UTC calendar day from the absolute instant — timezone-independent
    # (no session-conf mutation needed): floor(epoch_seconds / 86400) -> date.
    epoch_sec = F.unix_timestamp(F.col("event_ts"))
    df = df.withColumn(
        "event_date",
        F.date_from_unix_date(F.floor(epoch_sec / F.lit(86400)).cast("int")),
    )

    # Amount must cast to a non-null number.
    df = df.withColumn("amount_num", F.col("amount_raw").cast("double"))

    # Expectations: valid date, valid amount, present key.
    df = df.where(
        F.col("event_date").isNotNull()
        & F.col("amount_num").isNotNull()
        & F.col("order_id").isNotNull()
    )

    # Deterministic dedup on the business key (order_id), newest event wins,
    # tie-broken deterministically so reprocessing is stable.
    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(
        F.col("event_ts").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("amount_num").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("value").desc_nulls_last(),
    )
    deduped = (
        df.withColumn("_rn", F.row_number().over(w))
        .where(F.col("_rn") == 1)
        .drop("_rn")
    )
    return deduped.select("order_id", "event_date", "amount_num")


@dp.materialized_view(name="gold_daily")
def gold_daily():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    src = spark.read.table("spark_catalog.default.orders_clean")
    return (
        src.groupBy("event_date")
        .agg(F.sum("amount_num").alias("revenue"))
        .select(F.col("event_date").cast("date").alias("event_date"), "revenue")
    )

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, Window, functions as F

INPUT_PATH = "file:///home/lnc/repos/safe-spark-agents/.worktrees/dev-sweep/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work_seed_s2/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed2718.ndjson"

# Broad string-typed schema: type everything as STRING first, validate, then cast.
# Extra fields absent from a given JSON row simply parse to null -> coalesced away.
SCHEMA = (
    "order_id STRING, id STRING, "
    "event_time STRING, event_ts STRING, occurred_at STRING, ts STRING, timestamp STRING, "
    "amount STRING, revenue STRING, price STRING, total STRING"
)


def _epoch_seconds(col):
    # Epoch arrives as a numeric string in MILLIS; ISO strings parse to an instant.
    is_epoch = F.col(col).rlike("^[0-9]+$")
    return F.when(is_epoch, F.col(col).cast("long") / F.lit(1000)) \
            .otherwise(F.unix_timestamp(F.to_timestamp(F.col(col))))


@dp.materialized_view(name="bronze_orders")
def bronze_orders():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    raw = spark.read.text(INPUT_PATH)  # column: value
    parsed = raw.withColumn("j", F.from_json(F.col("value"), SCHEMA))

    flat = parsed.select(
        F.coalesce(F.col("j.order_id"), F.col("j.id")).alias("order_id"),
        F.coalesce(
            F.col("j.event_time"), F.col("j.event_ts"),
            F.col("j.occurred_at"), F.col("j.ts"), F.col("j.timestamp"),
        ).alias("event_raw"),
        F.coalesce(
            F.col("j.amount"), F.col("j.revenue"),
            F.col("j.price"), F.col("j.total"),
        ).alias("amount_raw"),
    )

    # UTC event-time normalization done with pure date arithmetic (no session tz needed):
    epoch_sec = _epoch_seconds("event_raw")
    days = F.floor(epoch_sec / F.lit(86400)).cast("int")
    typed = (flat
             .withColumn("epoch_sec", epoch_sec)
             .withColumn("event_date", F.date_add(F.to_date(F.lit("1970-01-01")), days))
             .withColumn("revenue", F.col("amount_raw").cast("double")))

    # Expectations: keep only rows with a valid UTC day and a parseable amount.
    clean = typed.where(
        F.col("event_date").isNotNull() & F.col("revenue").isNotNull()
    )

    # Deterministic dedup: one record per order_id, newest event time wins,
    # order_id as a stable tiebreaker so the same input always yields the same survivor.
    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(
        F.col("epoch_sec").desc_nulls_last(), F.col("order_id").desc_nulls_last()
    )
    deduped = (clean
               .withColumn("_rn", F.row_number().over(w))
               .where(F.col("_rn") == 1)
               .drop("_rn"))

    return deduped.select("order_id", "event_date", "revenue")


@dp.materialized_view(name="gold_daily")
def gold_daily():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    src = spark.read.table("spark_catalog.default.bronze_orders")
    return (src
            .groupBy("event_date")
            .agg(F.sum("revenue").alias("revenue"))
            .select(F.col("event_date").cast("date").alias("event_date"), "revenue"))

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F
from pyspark.sql.types import StructType, StructField, StringType

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Source for this seed (messy mobile order events).
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
DATA = (
    "file:///home/lnc/repos/safe-spark-agents/.worktrees/dev-sweep/"
    "experiments/safe_agent_study/.work_seed_s2/_data/"
    "gen_messy_orders_seed1337.ndjson"
)

# Candidate field names for the *occurrence* time (the day the order truly
# happened).  Ordered so that specific "true event" names win over generic ones.
EVENT_COLS = [
    "occurred_at", "occurred_time", "occurred",
    "event_time", "event_ts", "event_timestamp", "event_date",
    "happened_at", "sale_time", "order_time", "order_ts",
    "created_at", "time", "ts", "timestamp",
]

# Candidate field names for the monetary amount.
AMOUNT_COLS = [
    "amount", "revenue", "price", "total", "value",
    "amount_usd", "gross", "sales", "order_total", "sale_amount",
]

# Arrival/sync time columns we deliberately ignore (they are the *symptom*).
ARRIVAL_COLS = [
    "ingest_time", "ingested_at", "received_at", "synced_at", "sync_time",
    "arrival_time", "arrived_at", "upload_time", "server_time", "processed_at",
]

OTHER_COLS = ["order_id"]

# Build a permissive superset schema (all strings) so that fields that do not
# exist in this particular feed simply read as NULL instead of erroring, and
# numeric/quoted variants both parse.  We then coerce explicitly below.
_seen = set()
_all = []
for c in EVENT_COLS + AMOUNT_COLS + ARRIVAL_COLS + OTHER_COLS:
    if c not in _seen:
        _seen.add(c)
        _all.append(c)
SCHEMA = StructType([StructField(c, StringType(), True) for c in _all])


def _event_date(col):
    """UTC calendar day on which the order occurred, robust to messy encodings.

    Handles epoch seconds/millis/micros (purely arithmetic, timezone-free) and
    ISO-8601 date/timestamp strings (date prefix extracted directly), so the
    result is the true UTC day regardless of when the event arrived.
    """
    s = col.cast("string")

    is_num = s.rlike(r"^\s*[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?\s*$")
    digits = F.regexp_extract(s, r"([0-9]+)", 1)
    ln = F.length(digits)
    epoch_sec = (
        F.when(ln >= 16, digits.cast("double") / 1000000.0)
         .when(ln >= 13, digits.cast("double") / 1000.0)
         .otherwise(digits.cast("double"))
    )
    days = F.floor(epoch_sec / 86400.0).cast("int")
    date_from_epoch = F.date_add(F.lit("1970-01-01").cast("date"), days)

    iso = F.regexp_extract(s, r"(\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2})", 1)
    date_from_iso = F.to_date(iso, "yyyy-MM-dd")

    return F.when(is_num, date_from_epoch).otherwise(date_from_iso)


@dp.materialized_view(name="gold_daily")
def gold_daily():
    df = SparkSession.active().read.schema(SCHEMA).json(DATA)

    event_ts = F.coalesce(*[F.col(c) for c in EVENT_COLS])
    amount_raw = F.coalesce(*[F.col(c) for c in AMOUNT_COLS])
    amount = F.regexp_replace(amount_raw, r"[^0-9.\-]", "").cast("double")

    enriched = (
        df.withColumn("event_date", _event_date(event_ts))
          .withColumn("revenue_amt", amount)
          .where(F.col("event_date").isNotNull())
          .where(F.col("revenue_amt").isNotNull())
    )

    return (
        enriched.groupBy("event_date")
                .agg(F.sum("revenue_amt").alias("revenue"))
                .select(F.col("event_date").cast("date").alias("event_date"),
                        F.col("revenue"))
    )

from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F

DATA = "file:///home/lnc/repos/safe-spark-agents/.worktrees/dev-sweep/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work_seed_s2/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed2718.ndjson"


@dp.materialized_view(name="bronze_orders")
def bronze_orders():

    return SparkSession.active().read.json(DATA)


@dp.materialized_view(name="gold_daily")
def gold_daily():
    df = SparkSession.active().read.table("spark_catalog.default.bronze_orders")

    # --- Event time: the day the order ACTUALLY occurred (not arrival/sync time) ---
    # Robust, non-failing parse of several possible encodings. try_* => null on bad input
    # (avoids CAST_INVALID_INPUT). No session timezone is mutated.
    event_ts = F.coalesce(
        F.expr("try_to_timestamp(event_time)"),
        F.expr("try_to_timestamp(event_time, \"yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss[.SSS][XXX]\")"),
        F.expr("try_to_timestamp(event_time, 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss')"),
        F.expr("try_to_timestamp(event_time, 'yyyy-MM-dd')"),
        # epoch milliseconds encoded as a number/string
        F.timestamp_seconds(F.expr("try_cast(event_time as long)") / F.lit(1000.0)),
    )

    # Absolute instant -> epoch seconds (UTC). Casting a timestamp to long is
    # timezone-independent, so flooring to whole days yields the UTC calendar day.
    epoch_secs = event_ts.cast("long")
    utc_day_num = F.floor(epoch_secs / F.lit(86400)).cast("int")

    # --- Revenue: clean noisy money strings ($, commas, etc.) then safe-cast ---
    amount_clean = F.regexp_replace(F.col("amount").cast("string"), "[^0-9.\\-]", "")
    amount = F.expr("try_cast(amount_clean as double)")

    enriched = (
        df.withColumn("utc_day_num", utc_day_num)
          .withColumn("amount_clean", amount_clean)
          .withColumn("amount_val", amount)
          .withColumn("event_date", F.expr("date_from_unix_date(utc_day_num)"))
          .where(F.col("event_date").isNotNull())
    )

    return (
        enriched.groupBy("event_date")
                .agg(F.coalesce(F.sum("amount_val"), F.lit(0.0)).alias("revenue"))
                .select("event_date", "revenue")
    )
